message:
I spent some time digging into the basics of home fire safety recently, and honestly? A lot of it is refreshingly straightforward stuff we can all actually do. Turns out knowing a few key things ahead of time takes way less effort than I thought, and it genuinely helps you feel more ready. I found this solid resource from Ready.gov that walks through the practical side of things—no doom, just real information.

What's one fire safety habit you've already got down in your home, or something you've been meaning to tackle?
